title: podman play kube drops environment variables defined in a dockerfile
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

I'm running a pod with `podman play kube pod.yaml` and it seems like podman completely ignores my environment variables defined in the `Dockerfile` used to build the container used in the pod.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

Given the following `Dockerfile`:
```
FROM alpine

ENV FOO=bar
ENV BAR=baz
```
I build the image like this:
```
$ podman build -t foobar .
```
inspecting the image I can see my variables:
```
$ podman inspect foobar | jq .[0].Config.Env
[
  "PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin",
  "FOO=bar",
  "BAR=baz"
]
```
I have this pod definition file:
```
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  labels: {}
  name: foobar
spec:
  containers:
  - image: foobar
    name: foobar-alpine
    stdin: yes
    tty: yes
```
and now I run this:
```
$ podman play kube pod.yaml
$ podman attach foobar-foobar-alpine
# env
/ # env
HOSTNAME=foobar
SHLVL=1
HOME=/root
container=podman
TERM=xterm
PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin
PWD=/
```
my environment variables are not there.

I also confirmed that when running the container directly my variables are there:
```
$ podman run --rm -ti foobar
/ # env
HOSTNAME=cf5da7c7b9df
SHLVL=1
HOME=/root
container=podman
TERM=xterm
PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin
BAR=baz
FOO=bar
PWD=/
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.2.1
API Version:  2.1.0
Go Version:   go1.15.5
Built:        Tue Dec  8 15:37:50 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-2.2.1-1.fc33.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

comments.body: Looks like this was just fixed in the main branch. https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/8654
Thanks for the pointer, using the build in the COPR repo I can confirm it works.
